root_text.sn40.10:1.1: Atha kho āyasmā mahāmoggallāno—seyyathāpi nāma balavā puriso samiñjitaṁ vā bāhaṁ pasāreyya, pasāritaṁ vā bāhaṁ samiñjeyya; evameva—jetavane antarahito devesu tāvatiṁsesu pāturahosi.
root_text.sn40.10:1.2: Atha kho sakko devānamindo pañcahi devatāsatehi saddhiṁ yenāyasmā mahāmoggallāno tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ mahāmoggallānaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi. Ekamantaṁ ṭhitaṁ kho sakkaṁ devānamindaṁ āyasmā mahāmoggallāno etadavoca:
root_text.sn40.10:2.2: Buddhasaraṇagamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.
root_text.sn40.10:2.4: Dhammasaraṇagamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.
root_text.sn40.10:2.6: Saṅghasaraṇagamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjantī”ti.
root_text.sn40.10:3.2: Buddhasaraṇagamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.
root_text.sn40.10:3.3: Sādhu kho, mārisa moggallāna, dhammasaraṇagamanaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn40.10:3.4: Dhammasaraṇagamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.
root_text.sn40.10:4.1: Atha kho sakko devānamindo chahi devatāsatehi saddhiṁ …pe…
root_text.sn40.10:4.2: atha kho sakko devānamindo sattahi devatāsatehi saddhiṁ …pe…
root_text.sn40.10:4.3: atha kho sakko devānamindo aṭṭhahi devatāsatehi saddhiṁ …pe…
root_text.sn40.10:4.4: atha kho sakko devānamindo asītiyā devatāsahassehi saddhiṁ yenāyasmā mahāmoggallāno tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ mahāmoggallānaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi. Ekamantaṁ ṭhitaṁ kho sakkaṁ devānamindaṁ āyasmā mahāmoggallāno etadavoca:
root_text.sn40.10:5.2: Buddhasaraṇagamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.
root_text.sn40.10:5.4: Dhammasaraṇagamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.
root_text.sn40.10:5.6: Saṅghasaraṇagamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjantī”ti.
root_text.sn40.10:6.2: Buddhasaraṇagamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.
root_text.sn40.10:6.3: Sādhu kho, mārisa moggallāna, dhammasaraṇagamanaṁ hoti …pe…
root_text.sn40.10:6.4: sādhu kho, mārisa moggallāna, saṅghasaraṇagamanaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn40.10:6.5: Saṅghasaraṇagamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjantī”ti.
root_text.sn40.10:7.1: Atha kho sakko devānamindo pañcahi devatāsatehi saddhiṁ yenāyasmā mahāmoggallāno tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ mahāmoggallānaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi. Ekamantaṁ ṭhitaṁ kho sakkaṁ devānamindaṁ āyasmā mahāmoggallāno etadavoca:
root_text.sn40.10:8.1: “Sādhu kho, devānaminda, buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn40.10:8.2: ‘itipi so bhagavā arahaṁ sammāsambuddho vijjācaraṇasampanno sugato lokavidū anuttaro purisadammasārathi satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavā’ti.
root_text.sn40.10:8.3: Buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.
root_text.sn40.10:9.1: Sādhu kho, devānaminda, dhamme aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn40.10:9.2: ‘svākkhāto bhagavatā dhammo sandiṭṭhiko akāliko ehipassiko opaneyyiko paccattaṁ veditabbo viññūhī’ti.
root_text.sn40.10:9.3: Dhamme aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.
root_text.sn40.10:10.1: Sādhu kho, devānaminda, saṅghe aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn40.10:10.2: ‘suppaṭipanno bhagavato sāvakasaṅgho, ujuppaṭipanno bhagavato sāvakasaṅgho, ñāyappaṭipanno bhagavato sāvakasaṅgho, sāmīcippaṭipanno bhagavato sāvakasaṅgho, yadidaṁ cattāri purisayugāni aṭṭha purisapuggalā esa bhagavato sāvakasaṅgho āhuneyyo pāhuneyyo dakkhiṇeyyo añjalikaraṇīyo anuttaraṁ puññakkhettaṁ lokassā’ti.
root_text.sn40.10:10.3: Saṅghe aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.
root_text.sn40.10:11.1: Sādhu kho, devānaminda, ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgamanaṁ hoti akhaṇḍehi acchiddehi asabalehi akammāsehi bhujissehi viññuppasatthehi aparāmaṭṭhehi samādhisaṁvattanikehi.
root_text.sn40.10:11.2: Ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjantī”ti.
root_text.sn40.10:12.1: “Sādhu kho, mārisa moggallāna, buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn40.10:12.3: Buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.
root_text.sn40.10:13.1: Sādhu kho, mārisa moggallāna, dhamme aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn40.10:13.3: Dhamme aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.
root_text.sn40.10:14.1: Sādhu kho, mārisa moggallāna, saṅghe aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn40.10:14.3: Saṅghe aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.

root_text.sn40.10:43.4: Te aññe deve dasahi ṭhānehi adhigaṇhanti—
root_text.sn40.10:44.1: Sādhu kho, mārisa moggallāna, saṅghe aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn40.10:44.3: Saṅghe aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti …pe….
root_text.sn40.10:45.1: Sādhu kho, mārisa moggallāna, ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgamanaṁ hoti akhaṇḍehi …pe… samādhisaṁvattanikehi.
root_text.sn40.10:45.2: Ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.
root_text.sn40.10:45.3: Te aññe deve dasahi ṭhānehi adhigaṇhanti—
root_text.sn40.10:46.1: Atha kho sakko devānamindo chahi devatāsatehi saddhiṁ …pe…
root_text.sn40.10:46.2: atha kho sakko devānamindo sattahi devatāsatehi saddhiṁ …pe…
root_text.sn40.10:46.3: atha kho sakko devānamindo aṭṭhahi devatāsatehi saddhiṁ …pe…
root_text.sn40.10:46.4: atha kho sakko devānamindo asītiyā devatāsahassehi saddhiṁ yenāyasmā mahāmoggallāno tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ mahāmoggallānaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi. Ekamantaṁ ṭhitaṁ kho sakkaṁ devānamindaṁ āyasmā mahāmoggallāno etadavoca:
root_text.sn40.10:47.1: “Sādhu kho, devānaminda, buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn40.10:47.2: ‘itipi so bhagavā arahaṁ sammāsambuddho vijjācaraṇasampanno sugato lokavidū anuttaro purisadammasārathi satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavā’ti.
root_text.sn40.10:47.3: Buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.
root_text.sn40.10:47.4: Te aññe deve dasahi ṭhānehi adhigaṇhanti—
root_text.sn40.10:47.5: dibbena āyunā, dibbena vaṇṇena, dibbena sukhena, dibbena yasena, dibbena ādhipateyyena, dibbehi rūpehi, dibbehi saddehi, dibbehi gandhehi, dibbehi rasehi, dibbehi phoṭṭhabbehi.
root_text.sn40.10:48.1: Sādhu kho, devānaminda, dhamme aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn40.10:48.2: ‘svākkhāto bhagavatā dhammo sandiṭṭhiko akāliko ehipassiko opaneyyiko paccattaṁ veditabbo viññūhī’ti.
root_text.sn40.10:48.3: Dhamme aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.
root_text.sn40.10:48.4: Te aññe deve dasahi ṭhānehi adhigaṇhanti—
root_text.sn40.10:49.1: Sādhu kho, devānaminda, saṅghe aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn40.10:49.2: ‘suppaṭipanno bhagavato sāvakasaṅgho, ujuppaṭipanno bhagavato sāvakasaṅgho, ñāyappaṭipanno bhagavato sāvakasaṅgho, sāmīcippaṭipanno bhagavato sāvakasaṅgho, yadidaṁ cattāri purisayugāni aṭṭha purisapuggalā esa bhagavato sāvakasaṅgho āhuneyyo pāhuneyyo dakkhiṇeyyo añjalikaraṇīyo anuttaraṁ puññakkhettaṁ lokassā’ti.
root_text.sn40.10:49.3: Saṅghe aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.
root_text.sn40.10:49.4: Te aññe deve dasahi ṭhānehi adhigaṇhanti—
root_text.sn40.10:50.1: Sādhu kho, devānaminda, ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgamanaṁ hoti akhaṇḍehi acchiddehi asabalehi akammāsehi bhujissehi viññuppasatthehi aparāmaṭṭhehi samādhisaṁvattanikehi.
root_text.sn40.10:50.2: Ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgamanahetu kho, devānaminda, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.
root_text.sn40.10:50.3: Te aññe deve dasahi ṭhānehi adhigaṇhanti—
root_text.sn40.10:50.4: dibbena āyunā, dibbena vaṇṇena, dibbena sukhena, dibbena yasena, dibbena ādhipateyyena, dibbehi rūpehi, dibbehi saddehi, dibbehi gandhehi, dibbehi rasehi, dibbehi phoṭṭhabbehī”ti.
root_text.sn40.10:51.1: “Sādhu kho, mārisa moggallāna, buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn40.10:51.2: ‘itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavā’ti.
root_text.sn40.10:51.3: Buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.
root_text.sn40.10:51.4: Te aññe deve dasahi ṭhānehi adhigaṇhanti—
root_text.sn40.10:52.1: Sādhu kho, mārisa moggallāna, dhamme aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn40.10:52.3: Dhamme aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.
root_text.sn40.10:52.4: Te aññe deve dasahi ṭhānehi adhigaṇhanti—
root_text.sn40.10:53.1: Sādhu kho, mārisa moggallāna, saṅghe aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn40.10:53.3: Saṅghe aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.
root_text.sn40.10:53.4: Te aññe deve dasahi ṭhānehi adhigaṇhanti—
root_text.sn40.10:54.1: Sādhu kho, mārisa moggallāna, ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgamanaṁ hoti akhaṇḍehi …pe… samādhisaṁvattanikehi.
root_text.sn40.10:54.2: Ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.
root_text.sn40.10:54.3: Te aññe deve dasahi ṭhānehi adhigaṇhanti—
root_text.sn40.10:54.4: dibbena āyunā, dibbena vaṇṇena, dibbena sukhena, dibbena yasena, dibbena ādhipateyyena, dibbehi rūpehi, dibbehi saddehi, dibbehi gandhehi, dibbehi rasehi, dibbehi phoṭṭhabbehī”ti.

translation_text.sn40.10:1.1: And then Venerable Mahāmoggallāna, as easily as a strong person would extend or contract their arm, vanished from Jeta’s Grove and reappeared among the gods of the thirty-three.
translation_text.sn40.10:1.2: Then Sakka, lord of gods, with five hundred deities came up to Mahāmoggallāna, bowed, and stood to one side. Mahāmoggallāna said to him:
translation_text.sn40.10:2.1: “Lord of gods, it’s good to go for refuge to the Buddha.
translation_text.sn40.10:2.2: It’s the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.
translation_text.sn40.10:2.3: It’s good to go for refuge to the teaching.
translation_text.sn40.10:2.4: It’s the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.
translation_text.sn40.10:2.5: It’s good to go for refuge to the Saṅgha.
translation_text.sn40.10:2.6: It’s the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.”
translation_text.sn40.10:3.1: “My good Moggallāna, it’s good to go for refuge to the Buddha …
translation_text.sn40.10:3.6: It’s the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.”
translation_text.sn40.10:4.1: Then Sakka, lord of gods, with six hundred deities …
translation_text.sn40.10:4.2: Then Sakka, lord of gods, with seven hundred deities …
translation_text.sn40.10:4.3: Then Sakka, lord of gods, with eight hundred deities …
translation_text.sn40.10:4.4: Then Sakka, lord of gods, with eighty thousand deities …
translation_text.sn40.10:7.1: Then Sakka, lord of gods, with five hundred deities came up to Mahāmoggallāna, bowed, and stood to one side. Mahāmoggallāna said to him:
translation_text.sn40.10:8.1: “Lord of gods, it’s good to have experiential confidence in the Buddha:
translation_text.sn40.10:8.2: ‘That Blessed One is perfected, a fully awakened Buddha, accomplished in knowledge and conduct, holy, knower of the world, supreme guide for those fit for training, teacher of gods and humans, awakened, blessed.’
translation_text.sn40.10:8.3: It’s the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.
translation_text.sn40.10:9.1: It’s good to have experiential confidence in the teaching:
translation_text.sn40.10:9.2: ‘The teaching is well explained by the Buddha—apparent in the present life, immediately effective, inviting inspection, relevant, so that sensible people can know it for themselves.’
translation_text.sn40.10:9.3: It’s the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.
translation_text.sn40.10:10.1: It’s good to have experiential confidence in the Saṅgha:
translation_text.sn40.10:10.2: ‘The Saṅgha of the Buddha’s disciples is practicing the way that’s good, direct, systematic, and proper. It consists of the four pairs, the eight individual persons. This is the Saṅgha of the Buddha’s disciples that is worthy of offerings dedicated to the gods, worthy of hospitality, worthy of a religious donation, worthy of greeting with joined palms, and is the supreme field of merit for the world.’
translation_text.sn40.10:10.3: It’s the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.
translation_text.sn40.10:11.1: It’s good to have the ethical conduct that’s loved by the noble ones, unbroken, impeccable, spotless, and unmarred, liberating, praised by sensible people, not mistaken, and leading to immersion.
translation_text.sn40.10:11.2: It’s the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.”
translation_text.sn40.10:12.1: “My good Moggallāna, it’s good to have experiential confidence in the Buddha …
translation_text.sn40.10:15.1: and to have the ethical conduct that’s loved by the noble ones …
translation_text.sn40.10:15.2: It’s the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.”
translation_text.sn40.10:16.1: Then Sakka, lord of gods, with six hundred deities …
translation_text.sn40.10:16.2: Then Sakka, lord of gods, with seven hundred deities …
translation_text.sn40.10:16.3: Then Sakka, lord of gods, with eight hundred deities …
translation_text.sn40.10:16.4: Then Sakka, lord of gods, with eighty thousand deities …
translation_text.sn40.10:25.1: Then Sakka, lord of gods, with five hundred deities came up to Mahāmoggallāna,
translation_text.sn40.10:25.2: bowed, and stood to one side. Mahāmoggallāna said to him:
translation_text.sn40.10:26.1: “Lord of gods, it’s good to go for refuge to the Buddha.
translation_text.sn40.10:26.2: It’s the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.
translation_text.sn40.10:26.3: They surpass other gods in ten respects:
translation_text.sn40.10:26.4: heavenly lifespan, beauty, happiness, glory, sovereignty, sights, sounds, smells, tastes, and touches.
translation_text.sn40.10:27.1: It’s good to go for refuge to the teaching …
translation_text.sn40.10:28.1: It’s good to go for refuge to the Saṅgha.
translation_text.sn40.10:28.2: It’s the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.
translation_text.sn40.10:28.3: They surpass other gods in ten respects:
translation_text.sn40.10:28.4: heavenly lifespan, beauty, happiness, glory, sovereignty, sights, sounds, smells, tastes, and touches.”
translation_text.sn40.10:29.1: “My good Moggallāna, it’s good to go for refuge to the Buddha …”
translation_text.sn40.10:32.1: Then Sakka, lord of gods, with six hundred deities …

translation_text.sn40.10:32.3: Then Sakka, lord of gods, with eight hundred deities …
translation_text.sn40.10:32.4: Then Sakka, lord of gods, with eighty thousand deities …
translation_text.sn40.10:37.1: Then Sakka, lord of gods, with five hundred deities came up to Mahāmoggallāna, bowed, and stood to one side. Mahāmoggallāna said to him:

translation_text.sn40.10:38.2: ‘That Blessed One is perfected, a fully awakened Buddha … teacher of gods and humans, awakened, blessed.’
translation_text.sn40.10:38.3: It’s the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.

translation_text.sn40.10:38.5: heavenly lifespan, beauty, happiness, glory, sovereignty, sights, sounds, smells, tastes, and touches.
